dogs.name: Koda
dogs.notes: Senior dog — MAX 5 miles per day at relaxed pace

stays.name: TBD — Lake Garda Accommodation
stays.notes: TBD — pick location on lake (Riva del Garda / Malcesine / Sirmione)

days.title: Fly to Verona — Arrive Lake Garda

stops.name: Verona Villafranca Airport
stops.detail: Arrive, collect hire car, drive ~30 mins to Lake Garda.

notes: TBD — confirm flight times and hire car booking.
Drive from Verona airport to Lake Garda is ~30 mins (south lake) to ~1.5 hrs (Riva del Garda, north).

days.title: Sirmione & South Lake

stops.name: Sirmione Peninsula
stops.detail: Narrow medieval peninsula jutting into the lake. Walk the old town walls and lakeside promenade. Dogs OK outside the castle.

stops.name: Scaligero Castle
stops.detail: Well-preserved 13th-century moated castle. Leave dogs at a café terrace outside.

stops.name: Lazise
stops.detail: Charming walled village on the east shore. Dog-friendly lakeside walk.

eating.name: TBD — evening in Lazise or local

notes: Sirmione is very busy in August — arrive early (before 10am) to beat crowds.
Parking outside the old town gates; the centro storico is mostly pedestrianised.

days.title: Malcesine & Cable Car

stops.name: Malcesine Old Town
stops.detail: Prettiest village on the east shore. Castello Scaligero overlooks the lake. Narrow car-free alleys, lakeside cafés.

stops.name: Monte Baldo Cable Car
stops.detail: Rotating cable car to 1,760m. Panoramic views over the lake and Alps. Dogs allowed — check ahead for any restrictions.

notes: TBD — check cable car dog policy and book tickets in advance for August.
Malcesine is very popular — park at the edge of town and walk in.

days.title: Riva del Garda — North Lake

stops.name: Riva del Garda
stops.detail: Dramatic northern tip of the lake, hemmed in by cliffs. Piazza III Novembre, lakeside promenade, the Rocca fortress.

stops.name: Arco Castle
stops.detail: Ruined medieval castle perched above the town. Short steep walk up — stunning views. Dogs welcome in grounds.

eating.name: TBD — Riva del Garda piazza restaurant

notes: Riva del Garda is the windsurf and kiteboard capital of the lake — lively atmosphere.
The drive north along the east shore (SS249) is very scenic.

days.title: Limone sul Garda — West Shore

stops.name: Limone sul Garda
stops.detail: Tiny lemon-grove village clinging to steep cliffs on the west shore. Terraced citrus gardens, colourful harbour, narrow lanes.

stops.name: Gargnano
stops.detail: Quiet, authentic lakeside town — less touristy than most. Beautiful Art Nouveau villa gardens (Villa Feltrinelli).

notes: West shore road (SS45bis) is narrow and twisty in places — allow extra time.
Limone gets very busy in summer — park early.

days.title: Lake Day — Water & Relaxation
map_waypoints: TBD local beach or town, Lake Garda

stops.name: TBD — Lake Swimming / Beach
stops.detail: Relax day. Find a dog-friendly beach or lido. Many lakeside beaches allow dogs in quieter sections — check locally.

stops.name: TBD — Local town explore
stops.detail: Explore whichever nearby town hasn't been visited yet. Bardolino, Garda town, or Torri del Benaco are all good options.

notes: Flexible day — use for anything missed earlier in the week.
Pack bags in the evening for early departure on Day 7.

days.title: Return — Fly Home

stops.name: Verona Villafranca Airport
stops.detail: Return hire car, check in, fly home. TBD — confirm flight times.

notes: TBD — confirm flight times. Allow 2 hrs before departure for hire car return + check-in.
Verona city is worth a stop if there's time before the flight (30 mins from airport).
